steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Label the side pair for the named angle first: opposite, adjacent, and hypotenuse. Then pick the matching shortcut, such as SOH/CAH/TOA, complementary trig values, or a special right-triangle ratio.

steps.title: Use the 45-45-90 triangle ratio
steps.content: An isosceles right triangle is a 45-45-90 triangle with sides in the ratio $1: 1: \sqrt{2}$. If each leg has length $a$, then the hypotenuse has length $a\sqrt{2}$.

steps.title: Find the leg length from the hypotenuse
steps.content:
Since the hypotenuse is 58 inches:
$$a\sqrt{2} = 58$$
Rationalize to solve for $a$:
$$a = \frac{58}{\sqrt{2}} \cdot \frac{\sqrt{2}}{\sqrt{2}} = \frac{58\sqrt{2}}{2} = 29\sqrt{2} \text{ inches}$$

steps.title: Calculate the perimeter
steps.content:
Sum all three sides:
$$P = 29\sqrt{2} + 29\sqrt{2} + 58 = 58\sqrt{2} + 58 = 58 + 58\sqrt{2}$$
That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
